info.title: Margen Attack-Data API
info.description: Credit-metered API that delivers labeled deepfake attack-data (real vs AI-generated face images and their platform-perturbed variants). Data is organized into benchmarks (versioned datasets, e.g. synthetic-face-v1), each with its own queryable dimensions. Reverse-engineered from the live b2b-website routes and the proven scripts/data-api/margen_client.py; this spec is the source of truth for the generated SDKs. Auth: Bearer token (or x-api-key). Pull one image per credit; test keys pull a free fixed sample. The canonical path prefix is /api/v1/data; the unversioned /api/data prefix remains a permanent alias.

get.summary: List benchmarks the key can query
get.description: Returns the benchmarks visible to the key. Pick an id and pass it as the `benchmark` parameter on /catalog and /items.

get.summary: Dimensions and allowed values for a benchmark
get.description: Self-describing catalog for one benchmark: each dimension with its allowed values, plus a `filters` map of query-param to allowed values. This is the runtime source of truth for what a benchmark supports (the API is registry-driven; do not hardcode dimension values in a client).

Filterable list of items (ids + attributes, no storage paths). Any filter accepts a comma-separated list, which matches any of the values (OR within a dimension); separate params must all hold (AND across dimensions). Omit a dimension to include all of its values. Fixed dimensions validate and return 400 on an unknown value.

Dimension filters are benchmark-defined: the ones below cover synthetic-face-v1; call /catalog for a benchmark's actual dimensions and values. Additional per-benchmark dimensions are queried by their catalog `key`.

Pagination has three modes: (1) offset (default: `limit`/`offset`, exact `total`, `has_more`; no cursor), (2) cursor (`cursor` set: keyset, `total` null, `next_cursor` set), (3) lineage (`lineage=true`: pages whole lineages; `limit`/`offset` count lineages, `total_lineages`/`lineages` returned, no cursor).

The x-speakeasy-pagination extension on this operation describes cursor mode only: a generated SDK auto-pages by feeding `next_cursor` back into the `cursor` query param. Offset and lineage modes are paged manually.

parameters.name: skin_tone
parameters.description: Demographic cell skin-tone band(s), comma-separated. Values per /catalog (e.g. very_light,light,intermediate,tan,brown,dark).

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: real (genuine, unmodified) or fake (AI-generated). Comma-separated allowed.

parameters.name: perturbation
parameters.description: Condition(s) applied. Values per /catalog (e.g. clean,jpeg_q70,fb_pipeline). Alias: `condition`.

parameters.name: source_real_id
parameters.description: Pull the full lineage descended from one sourced real image.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Values above 500 are clamped; the response sets limit_clamped.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Keyset cursor from a prior response's next_cursor (cursor mode).

parameters.name: lineage
parameters.description: Set to true to page over whole lineages.

get.summary: Get a signed URL for one item
get.description: Returns JSON with a short-lived signed URL (not the image bytes). Fetch `url` with a plain HTTP client and NO auth header, within `expires_in` seconds. On the live tier this debits one credit before returning the URL; test items are free. Send an Idempotency-Key header so a retry of the same item is a no-op (not a second charge). On a zero balance returns 402 with code insufficient_credits.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Stable per item across retries so a retried download is not charged twice.

get.description: The key owner's tier and credit balance. Check before a large pull to avoid a mid-run 402.

bearerAuth.description: Authorization: Bearer <key>. Keys look like mgn_live_... (live) or mgn_test_... (free sample).

apiKeyHeader.name: x-api-key
apiKeyHeader.description: Alternative to the bearer header; same key.

Benchmark.name: benchmark
Benchmark.description: Benchmark id (e.g. synthetic-face-v1). Effectively required: omitting it works only while the key sees exactly one benchmark; once a second exists, omission returns 400 listing the available ids.

source_real_id.description: Lineage key; all variants of one source share it.

attributes.description: Benchmark-specific fields; {} when the benchmark has none.

dimensions.description: The queryable dimension keys for this benchmark (details via /catalog).

ItemList.description: Standard list envelope plus /items echo fields. total is null in cursor and lineage modes.

total.description: Exact count in offset mode; null in cursor/lineage modes.

applied_filters.description: Echo of the filters the query understood; null means the dimension was not filtered.

limit_clamped.description: True when the requested limit exceeded the max and was clamped.

lineages.description: Lineage mode only: lineages on this page.

values.description: Allowed values (labeled). Absent for lineage keys.

filters.description: Map of query-param to allowed values (self-describing).

url.description: Short-lived signed URL for one image (JPEG). Fetch with no auth header.

expires_in.description: Seconds until the signed URL expires (300).

balance.description: Credit balance after the debit; null for free test items.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. Branch on this, not on the message text.

param.description: The offending query param (invalid_param on an unknown fixed-dimension value).

allowed.description: Allowed values for the offending param (invalid_param).

BadRequest.description: Invalid request: unknown value for a fixed dimension (param + allowed), invalid cursor, or ambiguous benchmark (available listed).

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key.

InsufficientCredits.description: Insufficient credits (live tier), code insufficient_credits. Top up in the portal.

Forbidden.description: Key not permitted for this item (e.g. a test key requesting a live-tier item), code forbidden_tier.

NotFound.description: Item or benchmark not found (or not visible to this key).

RateLimited.description: Per-key rate limit exceeded. No Retry-After header is sent; back off with exponential jitter.
